options.A: per Examples 6-7 (Rossi/Gagne), post-exam discussion of general difficulty and personal performance is permitted, but discussion of SPECIFIC CONTENT (topics covered/not covered, formulas tested/not tested, question-specific details, attempts to determine correct answers) VIOLATES Standard VII(A).
options.B: Any post-examination discussion whatsoever — including general impressions of overall difficulty or one's own personal performance — violates the confidentiality obligations of Standard VII(A) once the testing window has opened.
options.C: Specific question content may be freely discussed once the next examination administration begins, because each new exam cycle renders the prior window's questions obsolete and releases them from confidentiality.

rationales.A:
Full statement: per Examples 6-7 (Rossi/Gagne), post-exam discussion of general difficulty and personal performance is permitted, but discussion of SPECIFIC CONTENT (topics covered/not covered, formulas tested/not tested, question-specific details, attempts to determine correct answers) VIOLATES Standard VII(A); candidates should avoid participation in threads that attempt to reconstruct exam content.

Correct. Per Examples 6 and 7: Example 6 (Rossi): 'Rossi did not violate Standard VII(A) when she wrote about how difficult she found the exam or how well she thinks she may have done. By revealing portions of the exam content covered on the exam and areas not covered, however, she did violate Standard VII(A).' Example 7 (Gagne): 'Gagne violated Standard VII(A) by providing confidential exam information, which compromises the integrity of the exam process. In trying to determine correct answers to specific questions, the group's discussion included question-specific details considered to be confidential.'
rationales.B: Incorrect. GENERAL difficulty and personal-performance impressions are permitted; only specific content is barred.
rationales.C: Incorrect. Question-specific confidentiality does not expire with the next administration.

options.A: Sharing exam recollections with test-prep providers is acceptable because it improves industry preparation.
options.B: per Example 8 (Pena/CFA4Sure), candidates and members should REFUSE to share exam content with test-prep providers or other third parties, even when solicited.
options.C: Refusal is required only when the provider offers payment for the content.

rationales.A: Incorrect. Exam content is confidential; supplying it to prep providers compromises integrity whatever the motive.
rationales.B:
Full statement: per Example 8 (Pena/CFA4Sure), candidates and members should REFUSE to share exam content with test-prep providers or other third parties, even when solicited; providing exam questions or content undermines the integrity of the exam; if the solicitors are themselves CFA Institute members or candidates, they also violate Standard VII(A) by seeking confidential information.

Correct. Per Example 8 Comment (Pena): 'Pena violated Standard VII(A) by disclosing exam questions. The information provided is considered confidential until publicly released by CFA Institute... If the CFA4Sure employees who participated in the solicitation of confidential CFA Program information are CFA Institute members or candidates, they also violated Standard VII(A).'
rationales.C: Incorrect. The refusal duty applies whether or not compensation is offered.

options.A: Misstatements to CFA Institute carry no professional consequence because the Institute is not a client.
options.B: Truthfulness duties to the Institute cover exam answers only, not administrative filings.
options.C: per Example 2 (Chiu), misrepresenting information to CFA Institute regarding any CFA Institute program (such as filing a fraudulent deferral request with altered documentation) violates Standard VII(A) AND Standard I(C) Misrepresentation.

rationales.A: Incorrect. Fraudulent representations to the Institute about its programs violate VII(A) directly.
rationales.B: Incorrect. Administrative submissions — deferral requests included — must be truthful.
rationales.C:
Full statement: per Example 2 (Chiu), misrepresenting information to CFA Institute regarding any CFA Institute program (such as filing a fraudulent deferral request with altered documentation) violates Standard VII(A) AND Standard I(C) Misrepresentation; candidates must be truthful in all communications and filings with CFA Institute.

Correct. Per Example 2 Comment (Chiu): 'By filing a fraudulent request for an Emergency Deferral, Chiu violated rules of the CFA Program and compromised the integrity of the CFA exam. As a result, her conduct violated Standard VII(A), in addition to Standard I(C) Misrepresentation.'
